sections.title: Reported speech: tense and modal changes

items:
We use reported speech when we want to say what someone else said.
eg *Laura said she was going to visit a new city she had heard about.*
If the reporting verb is in the past (eg *said*), we usually have to change the tense of what the person actually said.

rows: 'I **need** a travel adapter,' said Laura. → Laura said she **needed** a travel adapter.
'I**'m cooking** dinner for the whole group,' said Laura. → Laura said she **was cooking** dinner for the whole group.
'I**'ve booked** a table at the new restaurant,' said Laura. → Laura said she'**d booked** a table at the new restaurant.
'I**'ve been thinking** about studying abroad,' said Laura. → Laura said she'**d been thinking** about studying abroad.
'I **spent** a week in Florence,' said Laura. → Laura said she'**d spent** a week in Florence.
'I **was hoping** to get a window seat,' said Laura. → Laura said she'**d been hoping** to get a window seat.
'I'**d looked** at every hostel in the city before I found this one,' said Laura. → Laura said she'**d looked** at every hostel in the city before she found that one.
'I'**d been learning** Italian for months before I visited Rome,' said Laura. → Laura said she'**d been learning** Italian for months before she visited Rome.
'I**'m going to** try the local street food,' said Laura. → Laura said she **was going to** try the local street food.
'I**'ll** need a travel adapter,' said Laura. → Laura said she **would** need a travel adapter.
'I **can** drive everyone to the station,' said Laura. → Laura said she **could** drive everyone to the station.
'I **must** catch the early train,' said Laura. → Laura said she **had to** catch the early train.
'I **may** try the cooking class tomorrow,' said Laura. → Laura said she **might** try the cooking class tomorrow.

items.note: We do not need to make any changes to the verb tense or modal when we are reporting a scientific fact or when something is still true.

items.correct: 'Water **boils** at 100°C,' said Laura. → Laura said water **boils** at 100°C.

sections.title: Reported speech: pronoun and determiner changes

items:
With reported speech, we also usually have to change some pronouns and determiners.
These changes include:

rows: 'I've left **my** passport at the hotel,' said Laura. → Laura said she had left **her** passport at the hotel.
'I love **this** local market,' said Laura. → Laura said she loved **the/that** local market.
'**This** is a wonderful city,' said Laura. → Laura said **it** was a wonderful city.
'I love **those** mountain trails,' said Laura. → Laura said she loved **the/those** mountain trails.
'**These** are delicious pastries,' said Laura. → Laura said **they** were delicious pastries.
'I'm going to try **these**,' said Laura. → Laura said she was going to try **them**.

sections.title: Reported speech: time and place changes

items:
With reported speech, we also usually have to change words and phrases connected to time and place.
These changes include:

rows: 'I usually eat **here** when I travel,' said Laura. → Laura said she usually ate **there** when she travelled.
'I'm reading a travel guide **at the moment**,' said Laura. → Laura said she was reading a travel guide **then/at that moment**.
'I'm visiting the museum **tomorrow**,' said Laura. → Laura said she was visiting the museum **the next/following day**.
'I'm going to the food festival **tonight**,' said Laura. → Laura said she was going to the food festival **that night**.
'I'm starting my language course **next week**,' said Laura. → Laura said she was starting her language course **the following week**.
'I hiked up the mountain **yesterday**,' said Laura. → Laura said she'd hiked up the mountain **the day before/the previous day**.
'I visited that art gallery **last week**,' said Laura. → Laura said she'd visited that art gallery **the week before/the previous week**.
'I arrived here three days **ago**,' said Laura. → Laura said she'd arrived there three days **before/previously**.

items.note: We do not need to make any changes to time words/phrases when the information is still true at the moment of speaking/writing.

items.correct: 'I'm leaving for the airport **tomorrow**,' said Laura to Kai. → Kai immediately texted Nina and said, 'Laura said she's leaving for the airport **tomorrow**.'

items:
We use reported questions when we want to say what someone else asked.
eg *Laura asked Max if he wanted to join her on the hiking trip.*
We use the same rules regarding tense, pronoun and time and place word/phrase changes with reported questions as we do with reported speech.

rows:
have, do, be or modal
(ie yes/no questions)
'**Can you show me** the way to the station, Max?' asked Laura. → Laura asked Max **if/whether he could show her** the way to the station.

what, who, which, when,
where, why and how
what, who, which, when, where, why and how
'**Why did you choose this** restaurant?' asked Laura. → Laura asked me **why I had chosen that** restaurant.

items.note: In direct questions we use the question form and question marks (*Can you ...?*).
In reported questions we don't use the question form or question marks (*asked if he could ...*).

items: Different reporting verbs take different grammatical patterns. Some verbs can take more than one pattern.

content.subtitle: Examples
items: ✓ **deny** (verb + *that* clause) *Marco **denied (that)** he had cheated in the race.*

content.description: See page 197 for a full list of verbs and patterns.

sections.title: A: Tense changes in reported speech
sections.instructions: Write the verbs in the correct form.

items.sentence: Marcos said he (not / want) ...... any more coffee as he had already had three cups.
Claire said that she and David (see) ...... the new exhibition at the gallery recently.
When I rang, Amy said she (give) ...... her little sister a piano lesson so she asked me to call back later.
Pablo said he (try) ...... to get a table at that restaurant for months before he finally managed to book one.
Nina says she (decide) ...... to train for a marathon but I'm not sure she'll stick to it.
Our science teacher said that water (be) ...... the most important substance on earth.
Sam said he and Elena (be going to) ...... try the new Thai restaurant that weekend, but I don't know if they went.
Ryan said last night that he really (love) ...... cooking, even though he rarely has time to do it.
Jade said to Tom that she (ask) ...... the professor about the deadline on Friday, but actually she didn't.
items.answer: had asked/was going to ask/would ask

items.sentence: Lena told Craig she (bring up) ...... by her grandparents in the countryside, but in fact she grew up in the city.

sections.title: B: Modal changes in reported speech
sections.instructions: If the word or phrase in bold is correct, write OK. If it is incorrect, write the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: On Friday, my manager said I **can** work from home on Monday, but she changed her mind over the weekend.
The flight attendant said that if we needed assistance, we **will** have to press the call button.
Several students have said that Emma **may** get the highest mark in the class.
Harriet said **she'd been** thinking about changing her diet for quite a while.
Dan says he **would** send us the travel itinerary as soon as it is confirmed.
Sofia said she **has** to train for two hours every day to keep fit.
Before she got her results, Priya said that she **may** have to resit her biology exam, but now she knows she passed.
Did Professor Hall say why he **had** cancelled the seminar?
Rachel said she **should** arrive at the hostel by lunchtime tomorrow.
Finn said he **must** catch the last train to Edinburgh that evening, but apparently he missed it.

sections.title: C: Pronoun and determiner changes
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Zoe said ...... laptop had stopped working and asked if she could use mine to finish the report.
Your grandmother said that ...... old photographs in the attic might be worth a lot of money.
The coach told the players he had no choice but to drop .......
Lucas said to thank you for the olive oil and that ...... were the finest ones he'd ever tasted.
Before she left, Mia told her hosts that ...... had been a wonderful stay.
As soon as he saw the running shoes, Dan told us he absolutely had to buy .......
The Garcias said that ...... youngest son had just been accepted into medical school.
Grandma said that ...... painting on the wall had been done by her great-aunt.

sections.title: D: Time and place changes
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
'We'll be hiking in the mountains next month,' said Karl.
**following** Karl said that ...... be hiking in the mountains.

'I had Tom and Grace over for dinner last night,' said Jess.
**before** Jess said that ...... had Tom and Grace over for dinner.

'Marco came here two days ago and then suddenly left,' said Ian.
**gone** Ian said that Marco had ...... and then suddenly left.
items.answer: gone there two days before/gone there two days previously

items.sentence:
'We're going on our road trip tomorrow,' said Ben and Ava together.
**starting** Ben and Ava said together that ...... road trip the next day.

'I can pick you two up from the airport this afternoon,' said their uncle to Sam and Ella.
**that** Sam and Ella's uncle told his niece and nephew ...... up from the airport that afternoon.

'I'll get these, they're such a bargain!' said Rosa.
**going** Rosa said she ...... as they were such a bargain.

'You must train harder, Alex,' said Alex's coach.
**him** Alex's coach ...... train harder.

'I think you may be coming down with a cold,' James said to me.
**thought** James said that ...... be coming down with a cold.

'I don't know why they haven't contacted me recently,' said Fran.
**been** Fran said she didn't know why ...... recently.
items.answer: she hadn't been contacted/she hasn't been contacted

items.sentence:
'Everything was so different yesterday,' said Luke.
**been** Luke said everything ...... before.

sections.title: E: Cloze (Reporting verbs)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: The seminar room was quiet. 'So, let me be sure I understand the situation,' (1) ...... Professor Mills calmly.

After a moment, Nadia (2) ...... up, explaining that she (3) ...... love to have the data ready, but unfortunately her computer (4) ...... being repaired that week and she (5) ...... not have access to the files at all.
Felix was next. He looked apologetic and said that he (6) ...... meant to email the group his section by (7) ...... morning, but had completely forgotten.

Isla said she (8) ...... absolutely overwhelmed at (9) ...... point in the term, and said she (10) ...... not complete her part until she (11) ...... her feedback from the previous assignment at the end of the week.

Professor Mills looked at them all. She (12) ...... them how disappointed she (13) ...... in the group's progress, and that she (14) ...... be meeting each of (15) ...... individually before the next session.
The nurse asked me if .......
items.answer: I had had my ear pierced.

items.sentence: Marco wanted to know whether .......
items.answer: he could meet Doug there at six o'clock that night.
items.context: "Can I meet you there at six o'clock tonight, Doug?"

items.sentence: Rachel asked Tom and Sofia whether .......
items.answer: they were still moving to Blackpool the following week./they were still moving to Blackpool next week.
items.context: "Are you still moving to Blackpool next week?"

items.sentence: I asked Graham's supervisor if Graham .......
items.answer: has to wear a suit to work./had to wear a suit to work.
items.context: "Does Graham have to wear a suit to work?"

items.sentence: Kat asked me if .......
items.answer: I wanted someone to feed my cat while I was away./I wanted someone to feed my cat while I am away.
items.context: "Do you want someone to feed your cat while you're away?"

items.sentence: Petra asked Wendy if .......
items.answer: she could guess what she'd given Lindsay for her birthday./she could guess what she's given Lindsay for her birthday.
items.context: "Can you guess what I've given Lindsay for her birthday?"

items.sentence: Carlos asked Mia whether .......
items.answer: she loved him or not./she loves him or not.
items.context: "Do you love me or not, Mia?"

items.sentence: Ollie wondered whether .......
items.answer: Simon would be coming to the party the following night./Simon would be coming to the party the next night.
items.context: "Will Simon be coming to the party tomorrow night?"

items.sentence: The detective demanded to know whether Sykes .......
items.answer: had been anywhere near 34 Aylesford Street the previous night./had been anywhere near 34 Aylesford Street the night before.
items.context: "Tell me if you were anywhere near 34 Aylesford Street last night!"

items.sentence: My personal trainer asked me why I was thinking of quitting the gym.
items.answer: Why are you thinking of quitting the gym?

items.sentence: The student asked the lecturer what the difference was between a refugee and an asylum seeker.
items.answer: What's the difference between a refugee and an asylum seeker?

items.sentence: Our travel guide asked us how we had got on with Peter three days before.
items.answer: How did you get on with Peter three days ago?

items.sentence: The travel agent asked us when we had last been on holiday.
items.answer: When did you last go on holiday?

items.sentence: The chef asked her which of the dishes she preferred.
items.answer: Which of them do you prefer?/Which of these do you prefer?

items.sentence: My colleague asked me how I was going to get to Manchester tomorrow.
items.answer: How are you going to get to Manchester tomorrow?

items.sentence: Adam asked Bella who she had been out with the weekend before.
items.answer: Who did you go out with last weekend?

items.sentence: Bella asked Adam what gave him the right to ask questions like that.
items.answer: What gives you the right to ask questions like that?/What gives you the right to ask questions like this?

sections.title: H: Reporting verbs (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: If any student wants ...... the lecturer a question, please raise your hand.
The scientists ...... repeatedly in their published reports that the new vaccine is both safe and effective.
The captain ...... his crew to evacuate the vessel, which they did without hesitation.
...... me about your hiking experience in Patagonia. I'd love to hear every detail!
My flatmate still ...... to me for eating all my food without asking.
If you ...... to take the medication, the doctor said you would have got much worse.
The tour guide said she'd like ...... stopping at the viewpoint for a few minutes.
The athlete ...... having taken any banned substances, despite the positive test result.
I ...... to proofread your essay, not rewrite the whole thing for you!
Some online sellers ...... to offer genuine designer goods but actually sell counterfeits.

sections.title: I: Error correction (extra words)
sections.instructions: Circle the extra word in each line. Click it.

items.sentence: A classmate told to me last term that applying for a scholarship was straightforward.
When I rang the university, I asked if them how the application process worked.
The administrator refused [that] to give any details until I had completed the online form...
...before she could tell me whether I would have qualify for the international bursary.
I was frustrated, so I told it a small lie, saying that I had already been shortlisted by another institution.
She eventually said she would add my name to the waiting list, so that was a relief.
A week later, a friend told me [that] about a brilliant course at a different university.
She suggested to our applying together since the fees would be reduced for a group.
When I contacted that university and said so I was also interested in their scholarship scheme...
...the admissions officer denied doing that she had ever mentioned a scholarship being available...
...and claimed that she has had already reviewed my file and found I was ineligible.
I begged with her to re-examine my application as I clearly met all the criteria.
Finally, she agreed [me] to reconsider and sent me an email a few days later...
...which stated that the university would was pleased to offer me a full scholarship.
By then, it was said too late to accept, as I had already enrolled elsewhere!
